title: vaccinium uliginosum subsp. alpinum
intro: Vaccinium uliginosum subsp. alpinum, also known as Alpine Cranberry or Mountain Cranberry, is a subspecies of low-growing, evergreen shrub that belongs to the heath family, Ericaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Vaccinium uliginosum subsp. alpinum include reducing inflammation, treating digestive problems, and supporting the immune system. This plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat wounds and skin conditions.
